stories.title: With Every Free Clean, Window Washer Influencer Spotlights Great, Struggling Restaurants in Kansas City
stories.summary: Window washer Davis Roethler uses his social media skills to give free cleanings to struggling Kansas City restaurants, creating viral content that boosts their business. His unique approach helps save local eateries by telling their stories.
stories.extended_summary: Davis Roethler, co-owner of Window Wolf, offers free window cleanings to struggling restaurants while filming with his Meta glasses for social media. His Instagram videos don't just show the cleaning—they tell the story of the people behind the food, flipping traditional food content on its head. The exposure often leads to a surge in customers, helping businesses like Dunn Deal BBQ stay afloat. With nearly 9,000 followers and growing, Roethler's strategy proves that a squeegee and a smartphone can be powerful tools for community support.
stories.headline_split: With Every Free Clean, Window Washer Influencer Spotlights Great, Struggling Restaurants
stories.fake_headline: Teenager Builds Free Laundry Service for Homeless Shelters Using Solar-Powered Machines

stories.title: Growing Coffee in the World’s Densest City: Hong Kong Roastery Hails Beans That Aren’t Imported
stories.summary: Hong Kong's Lantau Island is now home to a working coffee farm, proving that premium Arabica can grow in the world's densest city. The Lantau Coffee Co-Op turns a passion project into locally grown pride.
stories.extended_summary: Ringo Lam, a former tech entrepreneur, planted 100 coffee seeds from Panama on Lantau Island—and 80 sprouted. Despite Hong Kong's lack of altitude and space, the Lantau Coffee Co-Op now produces Arabica beans at just under 1,000 meters elevation. Katie Chick, a university researcher, helps run a farm on Hong Kong island itself, yielding about 50 kilograms of beans from 800 trees. The operation challenges assumptions about urban agriculture and proves that with determination, coffee can thrive even in the most unlikely place.
stories.headline_split: Growing Coffee in the World’s Densest City:
fake_endings: Hong Kong's Urban Farm Produces First Batch
Local Rooftop Coffee Beans Hit Market
